method_atom: Exact computation of the full one-point probability distribution of a fluctuating scalar field driven by a multiplicative-noise linear stochastic PDE, whose logarithm obeys a nonlinear stochastic growth law. The marginal law is obtained in closed form as a crossover family that interpolates between a Gaussian distribution in the short-time regime and the GUE Tracy-Widom law in the long-time regime. The derivation proceeds by a rigorous steepest-descent asymptotic analysis of an exact determinantal/Fredholm formula for a discrete asymmetric interacting-particle system, taken in a weakly-asymmetric scaling limit under which the discrete model converges to the continuum equations.
object_atom: The free energy of the continuum directed random polymer in 1+1 dimensions, equivalently the height of the Kardar-Parisi-Zhang interface with narrow-wedge (delta) initial condition, and the asymmetric simple exclusion process of hopping particles with anti-shock initial data used as the solvable discrete model.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the exact-distribution / weakly-asymmetric-limit / steepest-descent machinery yielding a Gaussian-to-Tracy-Widom crossover law; the polymer free energy, KPZ interface, and exclusion-process particles are the domain-specific statistical-physics objects.
